fields.label: URL
fields.description: Specify a complete HTTP URL (for example https://example.com:8080)

overrides.description: Path to the CSV file on the Grafana server filesystem. Local storage must be enabled by an administrator via the GF_PLUGIN_ALLOW_LOCAL_MODE=true environment variable on the plugin process.

fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: Custom query parameters
fields.description: Add custom parameters to your queries.

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.js.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

The root.url field is dual-purpose depending on jsonData.storage. When storage='http' (default) it is the base HTTP URL of the CSV endpoint (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:82; pkg/http_storage.go:79). When storage='local' it is the filesystem path to the CSV file or directory used as a base by pkg/local_storage.go:33-45. Local storage requires the GF_PLUGIN_ALLOW_LOCAL_MODE=true environment variable on the plugin process (pkg/datasource.go:44,158-160).

instructions.msg: The CSV plugin has TWO storage modes, selected by jsonData.storage: 'http' (default) fetches CSV over HTTP against root.url + the query editor's per-query Path (pkg/http_storage.go:73-131); 'local' reads CSV from the filesystem at root.url — treated as a base path — plus the query's Path (pkg/local_storage.go:33-46). Local mode is admin-gated: the plugin process must be started with GF_PLUGIN_ALLOW_LOCAL_MODE=true (pkg/datasource.go:44,158-160). If not, the backend returns a downstream error 'local mode has been disabled by your administrator'. When storage is empty on load, both the frontend (src/utils.ts:4-10) and backend (pkg/settings.go:22-24) default to 'http' for backwards compatibility.

In HTTP mode, authentication uses the @grafana/plugin-ui Auth widget with default visibleMethods = [BasicAuth, OAuthForward, NoAuth] (auth-method/AuthMethodSettings.js:47-52). The editor's selector writes: 'Basic authentication' -> root.basicAuth=true + root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' -> jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' -> both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — convertLegacyAuthProps writes both flags on every selection (utils.js:36-48). CrossSiteCredentials (root.withCredentials) is recognised by getSelectedMethod but not offered in the default visibleMethods; a provisioning payload with withCredentials=true is preserved but not user-selectable in the editor. In 'local' storage mode the Auth widget is not rendered at all (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:77-112).

Minimal HTTP no-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:8080","jsonData":{"storage":"http"}}. Minimal HTTP basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://csv.example.com","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{"storage":"http"},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal HTTP oauth-forward payload: {"url":"http://csv.example.com","jsonData":{"storage":"http","oauthPassThru":true}}. Minimal local-file payload: {"url":"/var/lib/csv-data","jsonData":{"storage":"local"}}. root.url is required for storage='http' — pkg/datasource.go:121-125 CheckHealth rejects an empty URL with 'URL is required for HTTP storage'. For storage='local' the backend does not health-check the URL up front, but every query os.Stat's it (pkg/local_storage.go:48-50).

TLS is independent of the auth method and only applies to HTTP storage. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and requires jsonData.serverName plus secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/datasource.go:35-42 and are inert when storage='local'.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. Custom HTTP headers ARE now modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), exactly as @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component and the SDK HTTPClientOptions expect. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys. The CSV plugin itself defines no other plugin-specific secrets beyond the shared HTTP-settings set.

CSV-specific storage: jsonData.queryParams is a **string** of URL-encoded key=value pairs (e.g. 'limit=100&format=csv') appended to every outgoing HTTP query — pkg/http_storage.go:102-109 parses it with url.ParseQuery and merges it INTO the query editor's own params, so admin-configured query params override per-query params on key collisions. It is only meaningful when storage='http'. jsonData.storage itself is a simple string enum ('http' or 'local'); the backend PluginSettings.Storage (pkg/settings.go:11) matches it verbatim. Delimiter, header, decimalSeparator, skipRows, schema, ignoreUnknown, timezone, and the experimental.regex flag are all per-query (src/types.ts:9-28) and never live on the datasource itself.
